description: Trigger discrimination for organizing the whole docs SET by mode (this skill) vs the README entry point (readme-craftsman), the docs pipeline (docs-as-code-architect), and the onboarding set (onboarding-doc-designer).

cases.prompt: Sort our whole documentation set into tutorials, how-to, reference, and explanation, and fix pages that are the wrong type.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — classifying and structuring the whole set by the four modes is corpus organization. No single README and no toolchain is being built.
cases.reason: Shaping the documentation corpus by Diátaxis mode is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Write the project's README: what it is, who it's for, and a quickstart that runs.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the single entry-point front door is a README, not the corpus taxonomy.
cases.reason: readme-craftsman writes the entry point that links into the organized corpus; this skill organizes the corpus.

cases.prompt: Set up the docs build pipeline: generator, PR review, CI link-checking, and versioned deploys.
cases.reason: docs-as-code-architect designs the toolchain that builds/publishes docs. This skill organizes content by mode, independent of the build system.

cases.prompt: Design the onboarding docs for a new engineer joining the team — first-week setup, orientation, and a starter task path.
cases.reason: onboarding-doc-designer designs an audience-defined set for new joiners (spanning modes by need). This skill classifies the general corpus by mode; it doesn't design the onboarding journey.
